Decision: Write model.py/train.py/predict.py from scratch, train with L1 loss + logging to metrics.jsonl, save ckpt, submit_eval immediately for initial score and timing.

trajectory.description: Observation: Iter 1 wrote initial SchNet-based files but iter 2 command crashed with exit 139 (segfault, common with torch-geometric data loading / CUDA init / invalid tensor ops on QM9 3D). No eval score yet, time/iter budgets full. Analysis: Code likely has bugs in DataLoader (collate, batching pos/z), model forward, or missing pyg utils; must inspect before editing. Decision: Use command to cat all py files + check data/env, then in next iter fix bugs (e.g. proper PyG Data, Schnet config for regression, L1Loss, metrics logging), retrain short, submit_eval for baseline.

Observation: Inspected workspace files via command output (train.py, predict.py, model.py with SchNet+PyG on 3D QM9 data). Prior iter2 segfault (exit 139) during training, no eval score yet. Analysis: Likely root cause is DataLoader multiprocessing (num_workers=4/2) conflicting with PyG batching/CUDA init on this env; common PyG issue. Other code looks correct (mol_to_data, SchNet forward with z/pos/batch, L1Loss, metrics.jsonl logging, ckpt saving). Decision: Update model.py (fix import), train.py/predict.py (num_workers=0, batch_size=16 for stability/speed, epochs=5 on 20k subsample), run training for baseline, submit_eval immediately to get MAE score + measure full iter time before longer runs.

trajectory.description: Observation: Previous SchNet/PyG attempts repeatedly segfaulted (exit 139, empty stdout even on early prints). Analysis: Likely torch_geometric/CUDA/torch-scatter incompatibility in env; GNN imports or DataLoader trigger it. Decision: Switch to stable CPU baseline using RDKit Morgan fingerprints (2048 bits) + sklearn RandomForestRegressor on CSV data (avoids all GNN pkgs); subsample 20k for speed, log val_mae to metrics.jsonl, save via pickle; this will give first MAE score + timing measurement quickly before iterating to full data/better models (e.g. more trees, torch MLP on fps, or fixed GNN).

trajectory.description: Observation: RF on Morgan FP (2048bit,r=2) with n_est=30,max_depth=10 on full~105k train +2k val gives MAE 0.393 (val 0.406). Analysis: Underpowered model (low trees, capped depth); full val would give better estimate; SMILES FP methods can reach ~0.12-0.14 so room to improve before trying 3D GNNs or MLP. Decision: Update model.py for stronger RF (n_est=100, max_depth=None, n_jobs=-1, radius=3), clean/fix train.py to use full data/no unused subsample vars, retrain+log, submit_eval for improved baseline and timing.

trajectory.description: Observation: RF baseline achieved MAE 0.219 (val 0.219). Analysis: Morgan FP+RF is solid but limited capacity for QM9 electronic property; NN can capture nonlinear patterns better (expected MAE drop to ~0.16 range). Decision: Replace RF with torch MLP (2 hidden layers, BN, dropout) on same FP inputs; train 20 epochs with Adam+L1, log per-epoch val_mae to metrics.jsonl, save best.pt, submit_eval immediately for score/timing before further scaling (e.g. to 3D GNN or Mordred).

trajectory.description: Observation: MLP on Morgan FP (2048bit,r=3) reached val MAE~0.231 (eval 0.231), worse than prior RF baseline (0.219). Train loss decreased smoothly but plateaued above RF performance. Analysis: High-dim binary FPs suit tree ensembles better than small MLP; XGB typically outperforms RF on such regression tasks with similar features. Mordred too slow for 105k mols, 3D GNNs had prior segfaults. Decision: Install xgboost, rewrite model.py/train.py/predict.py for XGBRegressor(n_est=300,depth=9,lr=0.1,hist) on same FP featurization; full data, log val_mae, save as pkl; train+submit_eval for improved baseline and timing before further opts (e.g. more boosting rounds, hybrid feats, or GNN).
